    18	
    19	namespace MudBlazor
    20	{
    21	    /// <summary>
    22	    /// Represents a base class for designing form input components.
    23	    /// </summary>
    24	    /// <typeparam name="T">The complex type managed by this input.</typeparam>
    25	    /// <typeparam name="U">The value type managed by this input.</typeparam>
    26	    public abstract class MudFormComponent<T, U> : MudComponentBase, IFormComponent, IAsyncDisposable
    27	    {
    28	        private IConverter<T?, U?>? _defaultConverter;
    29	        private ConversionResult<T?>? _getConversionResult;
    30	        // ReSharper disable NotAccessedField.Local maybe we use it later, original converter didn't read the set errors
    31	        private ConversionResult<U?>? _setConversionResult;
    32	        // ReSharper restore NotAccessedField.Local
    33	        protected readonly ParameterState<bool> ErrorState;
    34	        protected readonly ParameterState<string?> ErrorIdState;
    35	        protected readonly ParameterState<string?> ErrorTextState;
    36	        private readonly ParameterState<CultureInfo> _cultureState;
    37	
    38	        [Inject]
    39	        private InternalMudLocalizer Localizer { get; set; } = null!;
    40	
    41	        protected MudFormComponent()
    42	        {
    43	            using var registerScope = CreateRegisterScope();
    44	            ErrorTextState = registerScope.RegisterParameter<string?>(nameof(ErrorText))
    45	                .WithParameter(() => ErrorText)
    46	                .WithEventCallback(() => ErrorTextChanged);
    47	            ErrorState = registerScope.RegisterParameter<bool>(nameof(Error))
    48	                .WithParameter(() => Error)
    49	                .WithEventCallback(() => ErrorChanged);
    50	            ErrorIdState = registerScope.RegisterParameter<string?>(nameof(ErrorId))
    51	                .WithParameter(() => ErrorId)
    52	                .WithEventCallback(() => ErrorIdChanged);
    53	            _cultureState = registerScope.RegisterParameter<CultureInfo>(nameof(Culture))
    54	                .WithParameter(() => Culture)
    55	                .WithChangeHandler(OnCultureAndFormatChangedAsync)
    56	                .WithComparer(ReferenceCultureComparer.Default);
    57	            registerScope.RegisterParameter<IConverter<T?, U?>?>(nameof(Converter))
    58	                .WithParameter(() => Converter)
    59	                .WithChangeHandler(OnConverterChangedAsync);
    60	        }
    61	
    62	        [CascadingParameter]
    63	        internal IForm? Form { get; set; }
    64	
    65	        /// <summary>
    66	        /// If true, this is a top-level form component. If false, this input is a sub-component of another input (i.e. TextField, Select, etc).
    67	        /// If it is sub-component, it will NOT do form validation!!
    68	        /// </summary>
    69	        [CascadingParameter(Name = "SubscribeToParentForm")]
    70	        protected bool SubscribeToParentForm { get; set; } = true;
    71	
    72	        /// <summary>
    73	        /// Requires an input value.
    74	        /// </summary>
    75	        /// <remarks>
    76	        /// Defaults to <c>false</c>.  When <c>true</c>, an error with the text in <see cref="RequiredError"/> will be shown during validation if no input was given.
    77	        /// </remarks>
    78	        [Parameter]
    79	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Validation)]
    80	        public bool Required { get; set; }
    81	
    82	        /// <summary>
    83	        /// The text displayed during validation if no input was given.
    84	        /// </summary>
    85	        /// <remarks>
    86	        /// Defaults to <c>"Required"</c>.  This text is only shown when <see cref="Required"/> is <c>true</c>.
    87	        /// </remarks>
    88	        [Parameter]
    89	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Validation)]
    90	        public string RequiredError { get; set; } = "Required";
    91	
    92	        /// <summary>
    93	        /// The text displayed if the <see cref="Error"/> property is <c>true</c>.
    94	        /// </summary>
    95	        [Parameter, ParameterState]
    96	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Validation)]
    97	        public string? ErrorText { get; set; }
    98	
    99	        /// <summary>
   100	        /// Raised when the <see cref="ErrorText"/> parameter value changes.
   101	        /// </summary>
   102	        /// <remarks>
   103	        /// This callback is triggered to support two-way binding of the <see cref="ErrorText"/> parameter.
   104	        /// The callback receives the updated <see cref="ErrorText"/> value.
   105	        /// </remarks>
   106	        [Parameter]
   107	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Validation)]
   108	        public EventCallback<string?> ErrorTextChanged { get; set; }
   109	
   110	        /// <summary>
   111	        /// Displays an error.
   112	        /// </summary>
   113	        /// <remarks>
   114	        /// Defaults to <c>false</c>.  When <c>true</c>, the text in <see cref="ErrorText"/> is displayed.
   115	        /// </remarks>
   116	        [Parameter, ParameterState]
   117	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Validation)]
   118	        public bool Error { get; set; }
   119	
   120	        /// <summary>
   121	        /// Raised when the <see cref="Error"/> parameter value changes.
   122	        /// </summary>
   123	        /// <remarks>
   124	        /// This callback is triggered to support two-way binding of the <see cref="Error"/> parameter.
   125	        /// The callback receives the updated <see cref="Error"/> value.
   126	        /// </remarks>
   127	        [Parameter]
   128	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Validation)]
   129	        public EventCallback<bool> ErrorChanged { get; set; }
   130	
   131	        /// <summary>
   132	        /// The ID of the error description element, for use by <c>aria-describedby</c> when <see cref="Error"/> is <c>true</c>.
   133	        /// </summary>
   134	        /// <remarks>
   135	        /// Defaults to <c>null</c>.  When set and the <see cref="Error"/> property is <c>true</c>, an <c>aria-describedby</c> attribute is rendered to improve accessibility for users.
   136	        /// </remarks>
   137	        [Parameter, ParameterState]
   138	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Validation)]
   139	        public string? ErrorId { get; set; }
   140	
   141	        /// <summary>
   142	        /// Raised when the <see cref="ErrorId"/> parameter value changes.
   143	        /// </summary>
   144	        /// <remarks>
   145	        /// This callback is triggered to support two-way binding of the <see cref="ErrorId"/> parameter.
   146	        /// The callback receives the updated <see cref="ErrorId"/> value.
   147	        /// </remarks>
   148	        [Parameter]
   149	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Validation)]
   150	        public EventCallback<string?> ErrorIdChanged { get; set; }
   151	
   152	        /// <summary>
   153	        /// The type converter for this input.
   154	        /// </summary>
   155	        /// <remarks>
   156	        /// This property provides a way to customize conversions between <typeparamref name="T"/> objects and <typeparamref name="U"/> values.
   157	        /// You can assign <c>null</c> to this property; in that case, the component will use the default converter returned by <see cref="GetDefaultConverter"/>.
   158	        /// </remarks>
   159	        [Parameter, ParameterState(ParameterUsage = ParameterUsageOptions.None)]
   160	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Behavior)]
   161	        public IConverter<T?, U?>? Converter { get; set; }
   162	
   163	        /// <summary>
   164	        /// The culture used to format and interpret values such as dates and currency.
   165	        /// </summary>
   166	        /// <remarks>
   167	        /// Defaults to <see cref="CultureInfo.InvariantCulture"/>.
   168	        /// </remarks>
   169	        [Parameter, ParameterState]
   170	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Behavior)]
   171	        public CultureInfo Culture { get; set; } = CultureInfo.CurrentUICulture;
   172	
   173	        protected virtual void OnConversionErrorOccurred(string error)
   174	        {
   175	            /* Descendants can override this method to catch conversion errors */
   176	        }
   177	
   178	        /// <summary>
   179	        /// Indicates a problem has occurred during conversion.
   180	        /// </summary>
   181	        /// <remarks>
   182	        /// When <c>true</c>, the <see cref="Converter"/> was unable to convert values, usually due to invalid input.
   183	        /// </remarks>
   184	        [MemberNotNullWhen(true, nameof(ConversionErrorMessage))]
   185	        public bool ConversionError => _getConversionResult is { Success: false };
   186	
   187	        /// <summary>
   188	        /// The error describing why type conversion failed.
   189	        /// </summary>
   190	        /// <remarks>
   191	        /// When set, returns the reason that the <see cref="Converter"/> was unable to convert values, usually due to invalid input.
   192	        /// </remarks>
   193	        public string? ConversionErrorMessage
   194	        {
   195	            get
   196	            {
   197	                if (_getConversionResult is not { Success: false } result)
   198	                    return null;
   199	
   200	                if (result.ErrorMessageKey is not null)
   201	                    return Localizer[result.ErrorMessageKey, result.ErrorMessageArgs];
   202	
   203	                return result.ExceptionError.Message;
   204	            }
   205	        }
   206	
   207	        /// <summary>
   208	        /// Indicates any error, conversion error, or validation error with this input.
   209	        /// </summary>
   210	        /// <remarks>
   211	        /// When <c>true</c>, the <see cref="Error"/> property is <c>true</c>, or <see cref="ConversionError"/> is <c>true</c>, or one or more <see cref="ValidationErrors"/> exists.
   212	        /// </remarks>
   213	        public bool HasErrors => ErrorState.Value || ConversionError || ValidationErrors.Count > 0;
   214	
   215	        /// <summary>
   216	        /// The current error or conversion error.
   217	        /// </summary>
   218	        /// <returns>
   219	        /// This property returns the value in <see cref="ErrorText"/> or <see cref="ConversionErrorMessage"/>.
   220	        /// </returns>
   221	        public string? GetErrorText()
   222	        {
   223	            // ErrorText is either set from outside or the first validation error
   224	            if (!IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ErrorTextState.Value))
   225	            {
   226	                return ErrorTextState.Value;
   227	            }
   228	
   229	            if (!IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ConversionErrorMessage))
   230	            {
   231	                return ConversionErrorMessage;
   232	            }
   233	
   234	            return null;
   235	        }
   236	
   237	        /// <summary>
   238	        /// Indicates whether the user has interacted with this input or the focus has been released.
   239	        /// </summary>
   240	        /// <remarks>
   241	        /// Defaults to <c>false</c>.  When <c>true</c>, the user has performed input, or focus has moved away from this input.  This property is typically used to show the <see cref="RequiredError"/> text only after the user has interacted with this input.
   242	        /// </remarks>
   243	        public bool Touched { get; protected set; }
   244	
   245	        #region MudForm Validation
   246	
   247	        /// <summary>
   248	        /// The list of problems with the current input value.
   249	        /// </summary>
   250	        /// <remarks>
   251	        /// When using a <see cref="MudForm"/>, this property is updated when validation has been performed.  Use the <see cref="Validation"/> property to control what validations are performed.
   252	        /// </remarks>
   253	        public List<string> ValidationErrors { get; set; } = new();
   254	
   255	        /// <summary>
   256	        /// The function used to detect problems with the input.
   257	        /// </summary>
   258	        /// <remarks>
   259	        /// When using a <see cref="MudForm"/>, this property can be any of several kinds of functions:
   260	        /// <para>
   261	        /// 1. A <c>Func&lt;T,bool&gt;</c> or <c>Func&lt;T,Task&lt;bool&gt;&gt;</c> function.  Returns <c>true</c> if valid.  When <c>false</c>, a standard <c>"Invalid"</c> message is shown.
   262	        /// </para>
   263	        /// <para>
   264	        /// 2. A <c>Func&lt;T,string&gt;</c> or <c>Func&lt;T,Task&lt;string&gt;&gt;</c> function.  Returns <c>null</c> if valid, or a string explaining the error.
   265	        /// </para>
   266	        /// <para>
   267	        /// 3. A <c>Func&lt;T,IEnumerable&lt;string&gt;&gt;</c> or <c>Func&lt;T,Task&lt;IEnumerable&lt;string&gt;&gt;&gt;</c> function.  Returns an empty list if valid, or a list of validation errors.
   268	        /// </para>
   269	        /// <para>
   270	        /// 3. A <c>Func&lt;object,string,IEnumerable&lt;string&gt;&gt;</c> or <c>Func&lt;object,string,Task&lt;IEnumerable&lt;string&gt;&gt;&gt;</c> function.  Given the form model and path to the member, returns an empty list if valid, or a list of validation errors.
   271	        /// </para>
   272	        /// <para>
   273	        /// 4. A <see cref="ValidationAttribute"/> object.
   274	        /// </para>
   275	        /// </remarks>
   276	        [Parameter]
   277	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Validation)]
   278	        public object? Validation { get; set; }
   279	
   280	        /// <summary>
   281	        /// This is the form component's value.
   282	        /// </summary>
   283	        protected T? _value;
   284	
   285	        protected Task BeginValidationAfterAsync(Task task)
   286	        {
   287	            Func<Task> execute = async () =>
   288	            {
   289	                var value = ReadValue;
   290	
   291	                await task;
   292	
   293	                // we validate only if the value hasn't changed while we waited for task.
   294	                // if it has in fact changed, another validate call will follow anyway
   295	                if (EqualityComparer<T>.Default.Equals(value, ReadValue))
   296	                {
   297	                    await ValidateValue();
   298	                }
   299	            };
   300	
   301	            return execute();
   302	        }
   303	
   304	        protected Task BeginValidateAsync()
   305	        {
   306	            Func<Task> execute = async () =>
   307	            {
   308	                var value = ReadValue;
   309	
   310	                await ValidateValue();
   311	
   312	                if (EqualityComparer<T>.Default.Equals(value, ReadValue))
   313	                {
   314	                    EditFormValidate();
   315	                }
   316	            };
   317	
   318	            return execute();
   319	        }
   320	
   321	        /// <summary>
   322	        /// Causes validation to be performed for this input.
   323	        /// </summary>
   324	        /// <remarks>
   325	        /// When using a <see cref="MudForm"/>, the input is validated via the function set in the <see cref="Validation"/> property.
   326	        /// </remarks>
   327	        public Task ValidateAsync()
   328	        {
   329	            // when a validation is forced, we must set Touched to true, because for untouched fields with
   330	            // no value, validation does nothing due to the way forms are expected to work (display errors
   331	            // only after fields have been touched).
   332	            Touched = true;
   333	            return ValidateValue();
   334	        }
   335	
   336	        protected virtual async Task ValidateValue()
   337	        {
   338	            // if there is an EditContext, there is no need for internal validation as it will get overwritten by 'OnValidationStateChanged'
   339	            if (EditContext is not null)
   340	            {
   341	                return;
   342	            }
   343	
   344	            var changed = false;
   345	            var errors = new List<string>();
   346	            try
   347	            {
   348	                // conversion error
   349	                if (ConversionError)
   350	                {
   351	                    errors.Add(ConversionErrorMessage);
   352	                }
   353	                // validation errors
   354	                if (Validation is ValidationAttribute validationAttribute)
   355	                {
   356	                    ValidateWithAttribute(validationAttribute, ReadValue, errors);
   357	                }
   358	                else if (Validation is Func<T?, bool> funcBooleanValidation)
   359	                {
   360	                    ValidateWithFunc(funcBooleanValidation, ReadValue, errors);
   361	                }
   362	                else if (Validation is Func<T?, string?> funcStringValidation)
   363	                {
   364	                    ValidateWithFunc(funcStringValidation, ReadValue, errors);
   365	                }
   366	                else if (Validation is Func<T?, IEnumerable<string?>> funcEnumerableValidation)
   367	                {
   368	                    ValidateWithFunc(funcEnumerableValidation, ReadValue, errors);
   369	                }
   370	                else if (Validation is Func<object, string, IEnumerable<string?>> funcModelWithFullPathOfMember)
   371	                {
   372	                    ValidateModelWithFullPathOfMember(funcModelWithFullPathOfMember, errors);
   373	                }
   374	                else
   375	                {
   376	                    var value = ReadValue;
   377	
   378	                    if (Validation is Func<T?, Task<bool>> funcTaskBooleanValidation)
   379	                    {
   380	                        await ValidateWithFunc(funcTaskBooleanValidation, ReadValue, errors);
   381	                    }
   382	                    else if (Validation is Func<T?, Task<string?>> funcTaskStringValidation)
   383	                    {
   384	                        await ValidateWithFunc(funcTaskStringValidation, ReadValue, errors);
   385	                    }
   386	                    else if (Validation is Func<T?, Task<IEnumerable<string?>>> funcTaskEnumerableValidation)
   387	                    {
   388	                        await ValidateWithFunc(funcTaskEnumerableValidation, ReadValue, errors);
   389	                    }
   390	                    else if (Validation is Func<object, string, Task<IEnumerable<string?>>> funcTaskModelWithFullPathOfMember)
   391	                    {
   392	                        await ValidateModelWithFullPathOfMember(funcTaskModelWithFullPathOfMember, errors);
   393	                    }
   394	
   395	                    changed = !EqualityComparer<T>.Default.Equals(value, ReadValue);
   396	                }
   397	
   398	                // Run each validation attributes of the property targeted with `For`
   399	                if (_validationAttrsFor is not null)
   400	                {
   401	                    foreach (var attr in _validationAttrsFor)
   402	                    {
   403	                        ValidateWithAttribute(attr, ReadValue, errors);
   404	                    }
   405	                }
   406	
   407	                // required error (must be last, because it is least important!)
   408	                if (Required)
   409	                {
   410	                    if (Touched && !HasValue(ReadValue))
   411	                    {
   412	                        errors.Add(RequiredError);
   413	                    }
   414	                }
   415	            }
   416	            finally
   417	            {
   418	                // If Value has changed while we were validating it, ignore results and exit
   419	                if (!changed)
   420	                {
   421	                    // this must be called in any case, because even if Validation is null the user might have set Error and ErrorText manually
   422	                    // if Error and ErrorText are set by the user, setting them here will have no effect.
   423	                    // if Error, create an error id that can be used by aria-describedby on input control
   424	                    ValidationErrors = errors;
   425	                    await ErrorState.SetValueAsync(errors.Count > 0);
   426	                    await ErrorTextState.SetValueAsync(errors.FirstOrDefault());
   427	                    await UpdateErrorIdStateAsync(HasErrors);
   428	                    Form?.Update(this);
   429	                    StateHasChanged();
   430	                }
   431	            }
   432	        }
   433	
   434	        protected virtual bool HasValue(T? value)
   435	        {
   436	            if (value is string valueString)
   437	            {
   438	                return !IsNullOrWhiteSpace(valueString);
   439	            }
   440	
   441	            return value is not null;
   442	        }
   443	
   444	        [UnconditionalSuppressMessage("Trimming", "IL2026:Members annotated with 'RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute' require dynamic access otherwise can break functionality when trimming application code", Justification = "In the context of EditContext.Model / FieldIdentifier.Model they won't get trimmed.")]
   445	        protected virtual void ValidateWithAttribute(ValidationAttribute attr, T? value, List<string> errors)
   446	        {
   447	            try
   448	            {
   449	                // The validation context is applied either on the '_fieldIdentifier.Model', `EditContext.Model`, or `this` as a stub subject.
   450	                // Complex validation with fields references (like `CompareAttribute`) should use an EditContext or For when not using EditContext.
   451	                // Prioritize more specific validation context of the input component (_fieldIdentifier) over the one from the more general, surrounding form (EditContext).
   452	                var validationContextSubject = _fieldIdentifier.Model ?? EditContext?.Model ?? this;
   453	                var validationContext = new ValidationContext(validationContextSubject);
   454	                if (validationContext.MemberName is null && !IsNullOrEmpty(_fieldIdentifier.FieldName))
   455	                {
   456	                    validationContext.MemberName = _fieldIdentifier.FieldName;
   457	                }
   458	
   459	                var validationResult = attr.GetValidationResult(value, validationContext);
   460	                if (validationResult != ValidationResult.Success)
   461	                {
   462	                    if (!IsNullOrEmpty(validationResult?.ErrorMessage))
   463	                    {
   464	                        errors.Add(validationResult.ErrorMessage);
   465	                    }
   466	                }
   467	            }
   468	            catch (Exception e)
   469	            {
   470	                // Maybe conditionally add full error message if `IWebAssemblyHostEnvironment.IsDevelopment()`
   471	                // Or log using proper logger.
   472	                errors.Add($"An unhandled exception occurred: {e.Message}");
   473	            }
   474	        }
   475	
   476	        protected virtual void ValidateWithFunc(Func<T?, bool> func, T? value, List<string> errors)
   477	        {
   478	            try
   479	            {
   480	                if (!func(value))
   481	                {
   482	                    errors.Add("Invalid");
   483	                }
   484	            }
   485	            catch (Exception e)
   486	            {
   487	                errors.Add("Error in validation func: " + e.Message);
   488	            }
   489	        }
   490	
   491	        protected virtual void ValidateWithFunc(Func<T?, string?> func, T? value, List<string> errors)
   492	        {
   493	            try
   494	            {
   495	                var error = func(value);
   496	                if (!IsNullOrEmpty(error))
   497	                {
   498	                    errors.Add(error);
   499	                }
   500	            }
   501	            catch (Exception e)
   502	            {
   503	                errors.Add("Error in validation func: " + e.Message);
   504	            }
   505	        }
   506	
   507	        protected virtual void ValidateWithFunc(Func<T?, IEnumerable<string?>> func, T? value, List<string> errors)
   508	        {
   509	            try
   510	            {
   511	                foreach (var error in func(value))
   512	                {
   513	                    if (!IsNullOrEmpty(error))
   514	                    {
   515	                        errors.Add(error);
   516	                    }
   517	                }
   518	            }
   519	            catch (Exception e)
   520	            {
   521	                errors.Add("Error in validation func: " + e.Message);
   522	            }
   523	        }
   524	
   525	        protected virtual void ValidateModelWithFullPathOfMember(Func<object, string, IEnumerable<string?>> func, List<string> errors)
   526	        {
   527	            try
   528	            {
   529	                if (Form?.Model is null)
   530	                {
   531	                    return;
   532	                }
   533	
   534	                if (For is null)
   535	                {
   536	                    errors.Add($"For is null, please set parameter For on the form input component of type {GetType().Name}");
   537	                    return;
   538	                }
   539	
   540	                foreach (var error in func(Form.Model, For.GetFullPathOfMember()))
   541	                {
   542	                    if (!IsNullOrEmpty(error))
   543	                    {
   544	                        errors.Add(error);
   545	                    }
   546	                }
   547	            }
   548	            catch (Exception e)
   549	            {
   550	                errors.Add("Error in validation func: " + e.Message);
   551	            }
   552	        }
   553	
   554	        protected virtual async Task ValidateWithFunc(Func<T?, Task<bool>> func, T? value, List<string> errors)
   555	        {
   556	            try
   557	            {
   558	                if (!await func(value))
   559	                {
   560	                    errors.Add("Invalid");
   561	                }
   562	            }
   563	            catch (Exception e)
   564	            {
   565	                errors.Add("Error in validation func: " + e.Message);
   566	            }
   567	        }
   568	
   569	        protected virtual async Task ValidateWithFunc(Func<T?, Task<string?>> func, T? value, List<string> errors)
   570	        {
   571	            try
   572	            {
   573	                var error = await func(value);
   574	                if (!IsNullOrEmpty(error))
   575	                {
   576	                    errors.Add(error);
   577	                }
   578	            }
   579	            catch (Exception e)
   580	            {
   581	                errors.Add("Error in validation func: " + e.Message);
   582	            }
   583	        }
   584	
   585	        protected virtual async Task ValidateWithFunc(Func<T?, Task<IEnumerable<string?>>> func, T? value, List<string> errors)
   586	        {
   587	            try
   588	            {
   589	                foreach (var error in await func(value))
   590	                {
   591	                    if (!IsNullOrEmpty(error))
   592	                    {
   593	                        errors.Add(error);
   594	                    }
   595	                }
   596	            }
   597	            catch (Exception e)
   598	            {
   599	                errors.Add("Error in validation func: " + e.Message);
   600	            }
   601	        }
   602	
   603	        protected virtual async Task ValidateModelWithFullPathOfMember(Func<object, string, Task<IEnumerable<string?>>> func, List<string> errors)
   604	        {
   605	            try
   606	            {
   607	                if (Form?.Model is null)
   608	                {
   609	                    return;
   610	                }
   611	
   612	                if (For is null)
   613	                {
   614	                    errors.Add($"For is null, please set parameter For on the form input component of type {GetType().Name}");
   615	                    return;
   616	                }
   617	
   618	                foreach (var error in await func(Form.Model, For.GetFullPathOfMember()))
   619	                {
   620	                    if (!IsNullOrEmpty(error))
   621	                    {
   622	                        errors.Add(error);
   623	                    }
   624	                }
   625	            }
   626	            catch (Exception e)
   627	            {
   628	                errors.Add("Error in validation func: " + e.Message);
   629	            }
   630	        }
   631	
   632	        /// <summary>
   633	        /// Notify the Form that a field has changed if SubscribeToParentForm is true
   634	        /// </summary>
   635	        protected void FieldChanged(object? newValue)
   636	        {
   637	            if (SubscribeToParentForm)
   638	            {
   639	                Form?.FieldChanged(this, newValue);
   640	            }
   641	        }
   642	
   643	        /// <summary>
   644	        /// Clears the input and any validation errors.
   645	        /// </summary>
   646	        /// <remarks>
   647	        /// When called, the <c>Value</c>, <see cref="Error"/>, <see cref="ErrorText"/>, and <see cref="ValidationErrors"/> properties are all reset.
   648	        /// </remarks>
   649	        public async Task ResetAsync()
   650	        {
   651	            await ResetValueAsync();
   652	            await ResetValidationAsync();
   653	        }
   654	
   655	        protected virtual async Task ResetValueAsync()
   656	        {
   657	            /* to be overridden */
   658	            await SetValueCoreAsync(default);
   659	            Touched = false;
   660	            await InvokeAsync(StateHasChanged);
   661	        }
   662	
   663	        /// <summary>
   664	        /// Clears any validation errors.
   665	        /// </summary>
   666	        /// <remarks>
   667	        /// When called, the <see cref="Error"/>, <see cref="ErrorText"/>, and <see cref="ValidationErrors"/> properties are all reset.
   668	        /// </remarks>
   669	        public virtual async Task ResetValidationAsync()
   670	        {
   671	            await ErrorState.SetValueAsync(false);
   672	            ValidationErrors.Clear();
   673	            await ErrorTextState.SetValueAsync(null);
   674	            await UpdateErrorIdStateAsync(false);
   675	            ResetConverterErrors();
   676	            await InvokeAsync(StateHasChanged);
   677	        }
   678	
   679	        #endregion
   680	
   681	        #region --> Blazor EditForm validation support
   682	
   683	        /// <summary>
   684	        /// The context used to perform validation.
   685	        /// </summary>
   686	        /// <remarks>
   687	        /// When using an <see cref="EditForm"/>, gets a context used to perform validation.
   688	        /// </remarks>
   689	        [CascadingParameter]
   690	        private EditContext? EditContext { get; set; }
   691	
   692	        /// <summary>
   693	        /// Triggers field to be validated.
   694	        /// </summary>
   695	        internal void EditFormValidate()
   696	        {
   697	            if (!IsNullOrEmpty(_fieldIdentifier.FieldName))
   698	            {
   699	                EditContext?.NotifyFieldChanged(_fieldIdentifier);
   700	            }
   701	        }
   702	
   703	        /// <summary>
   704	        /// The model field containing validation attributes.
   705	        /// </summary>
   706	        /// <remarks>
   707	        /// When using an <see cref="EditForm"/>, this property is used to find data annotation validation attributes such as <see cref="MaxLengthAttribute"/> used to perform validation.
   708	        /// </remarks>
   709	        [Parameter]
   710	        [Category(CategoryTypes.FormComponent.Validation)]
   711	        public Expression<Func<T>>? For { get; set; }
   712	
   713	        /// <summary>
   714	        /// Indicates whether the <see cref="For"/> property is <c>null</c>.
   715	        /// </summary>
   716	        [MemberNotNullWhen(false, nameof(For))]
   717	        public bool IsForNull => For is null;
   718	
   719	        /// <summary>
   720	        /// Stores the list of validation attributes attached to the property targeted by <seealso cref="For"/>. If <seealso cref="For"/> is null, this property is null too.
   721	        /// </summary>
   722	        private IEnumerable<ValidationAttribute>? _validationAttrsFor;
   723	
   724	        private async void OnValidationStateChanged(object? sender, ValidationStateChangedEventArgs e)
   725	        {
   726	            try
   727	            {
   728	                if (EditContext is not null && !_fieldIdentifier.Equals(default(FieldIdentifier)))
   729	                {
   730	                    var errorMessages = EditContext.GetValidationMessages(_fieldIdentifier).ToArray();
   731	                    var hasError = errorMessages.Length > 0;
   732	                    //TODO: v9 there no async API, but just make it async void (acceptable for EventHandler) 
   733	                    await ErrorState.SetValueAsync(hasError);
   734	                    await ErrorTextState.SetValueAsync(hasError ? errorMessages[0] : null);
   735	                    await UpdateErrorIdStateAsync(hasError);
   736	
   737	                    ValidationErrors.Clear();
   738	                    ValidationErrors.AddRange(errorMessages);
   739	
   740	                    await InvokeAsync(StateHasChanged);
   741	                }
   742	            }
   743	            catch (Exception exception)
   744	            {
   745	                Logger.LogError(exception, "An unexpected exception occurred: {ExceptionMessage}", exception.Message);
   746	            }
   747	        }
   748	
   749	        private Task UpdateErrorIdStateAsync(bool hasErrors)
   750	        {
   751	            var errorId = hasErrors
   752	                ? ErrorIdState.RenderValue ?? ErrorIdState.Value ?? Guid.NewGuid().ToString()
   753	                : ErrorIdState.RenderValue;
   754	
   755	            return ErrorIdState.SetValueAsync(errorId);
   756	        }
   757	
   758	        /// <summary>
   759	        /// Points to a field of the model for which validation messages should be displayed.
   760	        /// </summary>
   761	        private FieldIdentifier _fieldIdentifier;
   762	
   763	        /// <summary>
   764	        /// To find out whether or not For parameter has changed we keep a separate reference
   765	        /// </summary>
   766	        private Expression<Func<T>>? _currentFor;
   767	
   768	        /// <summary>
   769	        /// To find out whether or not EditContext parameter has changed we keep a separate reference
   770	        /// </summary>
   771	        private EditContext? _currentEditContext;
   772	
   773	        protected override void OnParametersSet()
   774	        {
   775	            base.OnParametersSet();
   776	
   777	            InjectCultureAndFormatToConverter(GetCulture, GetFormat);
   778	            if (For is not null && For != _currentFor)
   779	            {
   780	                // if there is an EditContext, there is no need for internal validation as it will get overwritten by 'OnValidationStateChanged'
   781	                if (EditContext is null)
   782	                {
   783	                    // Extract validation attributes
   784	                    // Sourced from https://stackoverflow.com/a/43076222/4839162
   785	                    // and also https://stackoverflow.com/questions/59407225/getting-a-custom-attribute-from-a-property-using-an-expression
   786	                    var expression = (MemberExpression)For.Body;
   787	
   788	                    // Currently we have no solution for this which is trimming incompatible
   789	                    // A possible solution is to use source gen
   790	#pragma warning disable IL2075
   791	                    var propertyInfo = expression.Expression?.Type.GetProperty(expression.Member.Name);
   792	#pragma warning restore IL2075
   793	                    _validationAttrsFor = propertyInfo?.GetCustomAttributes(typeof(ValidationAttribute), true).Cast<ValidationAttribute>();
   794	                }
   795	
   796	                _fieldIdentifier = FieldIdentifier.Create(For);
   797	                _currentFor = For;
   798	            }
   799	
   800	            if (EditContext is not null && EditContext != _currentEditContext)
   801	            {
   802	                DetachValidationStateChangedListener();
   803	                EditContext.OnValidationStateChanged += OnValidationStateChanged;
   804	                _currentEditContext = EditContext;
   805	            }
   806	        }
   807	
   808	        private void DetachValidationStateChangedListener()
   809	        {
   810	            if (_currentEditContext is not null)
   811	            {
   812	                _currentEditContext.OnValidationStateChanged -= OnValidationStateChanged;
   813	            }
   814	        }
   815	
   816	        #endregion
   817	
   818	        protected override Task OnInitializedAsync()
   819	        {
   820	            RegisterAsFormComponent();
   821	            return base.OnInitializedAsync();
   822	        }
   823	
   824	        protected virtual void RegisterAsFormComponent()
   825	        {
   826	            if (SubscribeToParentForm)
   827	            {
   828	                Form?.Add(this);
   829	            }
   830	        }
   831	
   832	        /// <summary>
   833	        /// Sets the culture used for parsing and formatting values in this component.
   834	        /// </summary>
   835	        /// <remarks>
   836	        /// This method updates the <see cref="Culture"/> parameter.
   837	        /// The converter will automatically use the latest culture when performing conversions.
   838	        /// If the converter supports <see cref="ICultureAwareConverter"/>, it will be updated with the new culture and format after the state change.
   839	        /// </remarks>
   840	        /// <param name="newCultureInfo">The new <see cref="CultureInfo"/> to use for this component. Must not be <c>null</c>.</param>
   841	        /// <returns>
   842	        /// A <see cref="Task"/> representing the asynchronous operation of updating the culture.
   843	        /// </returns>
   844	        /// <exception cref="ArgumentNullException">
   845	        /// Thrown if <paramref name="newCultureInfo"/> is <c>null</c>.
   846	        /// </exception>
   847	        protected async Task SetCultureAsync(CultureInfo newCultureInfo)
   848	        {
   849	            ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(newCultureInfo);
   850	
   851	            await _cultureState.SetValueAsync(newCultureInfo);
   852	            InjectCultureAndFormatToConverter(GetCulture, GetFormat);
   853	        }
   854	
   855	        /// <summary>
   856	        /// Called when the <see cref="Culture"/> or format string changes.
   857	        /// </summary>
   858	        /// <remarks>
   859	        /// Override this method in derived components to react to changes in culture or format settings.
   860	        /// The default implementation updates the converter with the latest culture and format,
   861	        /// but only if the converter implements <see cref="ICultureAwareConverter"/>.
   862	        /// This method is typically invoked automatically when the <see cref="Culture"/> or format parameter changes,
   863	        /// allowing components to refresh parsing and formatting logic as needed.
   864	        /// </remarks>
   865	        /// <returns>
   866	        /// A <see cref="Task"/> representing the asynchronous operation.
   867	        /// </returns>
   868	        protected virtual Task OnCultureAndFormatChangedAsync()
   869	        {
   870	            InjectCultureAndFormatToConverter(GetCulture, GetFormat);
   871	            return Task.CompletedTask;
   872	        }
   873	
   874	        /// <summary>
   875	        /// Called when the <see cref="Converter"/> parameter changes.
   876	        /// </summary>
   877	        /// <remarks>
   878	        /// Override this method in derived components to react to changes in the converter.
   879	        /// The default implementation updates the converter with the latest culture and format,
   880	        /// but only if the converter implements <see cref="ICultureAwareConverter"/>.
   881	        /// This method is typically invoked automatically when the <see cref="Converter"/> parameter is set or updated,
   882	        /// allowing components to refresh parsing and formatting logic as needed.
   883	        /// </remarks>
   884	        /// <returns>
   885	        /// A <see cref="Task"/> representing the asynchronous operation.
   886	        /// </returns>
   887	        protected virtual Task OnConverterChangedAsync()
   888	        {
   889	            InjectCultureAndFormatToConverter(GetCulture, GetFormat);
   890	            return Task.CompletedTask;
   891	        }
   892	
   893	        /// <summary>
   894	        /// Returns the <see cref="CultureInfo"/> used for parsing and formatting values in this component.
   895	        /// </summary>
   896	        /// <remarks>
   897	        /// Override this method in derived components to provide a custom culture for value conversion and display.
   898	        /// The culture is typically used by the converter (see <see cref="IConverter{T, U}"/> with <see cref="ICultureAwareConverter"/>) 
   899	        /// to control how values are parsed from and formatted to strings, such as date, time, or numeric formats.
   900	        /// <para>
   901	        /// By default, this method returns the value of the <see cref="Culture"/> parameter, which is tracked by the component's state.
   902	        /// </para>
   903	        /// </remarks>
   904	        /// <returns>
   905	        /// The <see cref="CultureInfo"/> to be used for parsing and formatting values.
   906	        /// </returns>
   907	        protected virtual CultureInfo GetCulture() => _cultureState.Value;
   908	
   909	        /// <summary>
   910	        /// Returns the format string used for parsing and formatting values in this component.
   911	        /// </summary>
   912	        /// <remarks>
   913	        /// Override this method in derived components to provide a custom format string for value conversion and display.
   914	        /// The format string is typically used by the converter (see <see cref="IConverter{T, U}"/> with <see cref="ICultureAwareConverter"/>) 
   915	        /// to control how values are parsed from and formatted to strings, such as date, time, or numeric formats.
   916	        /// <para>
   917	        /// By default, this method returns <c>null</c>, which means the converter will use its default formatting behavior.
   918	        /// </para>
   919	        /// </remarks>
   920	        /// <returns>
   921	        /// A format string to be used for parsing and formatting values, or <c>null</c> to use the default format.
   922	        /// </returns>
   923	        protected virtual string? GetFormat() => null;
   924	
   925	        /// <summary>
   926	        /// Returns the default converter used to convert between the component's model type <typeparamref name="T"/> and value type <typeparamref name="U"/>.
   927	        /// </summary>
   928	        /// <remarks>
   929	        /// <para>
   930	        /// This method is used when no custom <see cref="Converter"/> is provided (that is, when <see cref="Converter"/> is <c>null</c>). Derived components must
   931	        /// override this method to supply an appropriate default converter for their value type. The converter is responsible for converting
   932	        /// between the model value and the input value, including parsing, formatting, and handling culture-specific conversions if needed via
   933	        /// <see cref="ICultureAwareConverter"/>.
   934	        /// </para>
   935	        /// <para>
   936	        /// This method is called at most once per component instance: it is invoked the first time a default converter is needed (when
   937	        /// <see cref="Converter"/> is <c>null</c>), and the returned converter is cached and reused for all subsequent operations. Do not include
   938	        /// conditional logic that depends on per-call state or parameters.
   939	        /// </para>
   940	        /// </remarks>
   941	        /// <returns>
   942	        /// An <see cref="IConverter{T, U}"/> instance that performs the default conversion for this component.
   943	        /// </returns>
   944	        protected abstract IConverter<T?, U?> GetDefaultConverter();
   945	
   946	        /// <summary>
   947	        /// Converts the specified input value of type <typeparamref name="U"/> to the model value of type <typeparamref name="T"/>.
   948	        /// </summary>
   949	        /// <remarks>
   950	        /// This method uses the current <see cref="Converter"/> (or the default converter <see cref="GetDefaultConverter"/> if none is set) to convert the input value
   951	        /// from the component's value type to its model type. If conversion fails, the error is captured and can be accessed via
   952	        /// <see cref="ConversionError"/> and <see cref="ConversionErrorMessage"/>.
   953	        /// </remarks>
   954	        /// <param name="input">The input value to convert.</param>
   955	        /// <returns>
   956	        /// The converted value of type <typeparamref name="T"/>, or <c>null</c> if conversion fails.
   957	        /// </returns>
   958	        /// <exception cref="InvalidOperationException">
   959	        /// Thrown if <see cref="GetDefaultConverter"/> returns <c>null</c>, which indicates a programming error in the derived component class.
   960	        /// </exception>
   961	        protected virtual T? ConvertGet(U? input)
   962	        {
   963	            var converter = GetConverter();
   964	            var result = converter.TryConvertBack(input);
   965	            _getConversionResult = result;
   966	            HandleConversionResult(result);
   967	
   968	            return result.Value;
   969	        }
   970	
   971	        /// <summary>
   972	        /// Converts the specified model value of type <typeparamref name="T"/> to the component's value type <typeparamref name="U"/>.
   973	        /// </summary>
   974	        /// <remarks>
   975	        /// This method uses the current <see cref="Converter"/> (or the default converter from <see cref="GetDefaultConverter"/> if none is set) to convert the model value
   976	        /// to the component's value type. If conversion fails, the error is captured and can be accessed via
   977	        /// <see cref="ConversionError"/> and <see cref="ConversionErrorMessage"/>.
   978	        /// </remarks>
   979	        /// <param name="input">The model value to convert.</param>
   980	        /// <returns>
   981	        /// The converted value of type <typeparamref name="U"/>, or <c>null</c> if conversion fails.
   982	        /// </returns>
   983	        /// <exception cref="InvalidOperationException">
   984	        /// Thrown if <see cref="GetDefaultConverter"/> returns <c>null</c>, which indicates a programming error in the derived component class.
   985	        /// </exception>
   986	        protected virtual U? ConvertSet(T? input)
   987	        {
   988	            var converter = GetConverter();
   989	            var result = converter.TryConvert(input);
   990	            _setConversionResult = result;
   991	            HandleConversionResult(result);
   992	
   993	            return result.Value;
   994	        }
   995	
   996	        /// <summary>
   997	        /// Gets the converter used to convert between the component's model type <typeparamref name="T"/> and value type <typeparamref name="U"/>.
   998	        /// </summary>
   999	        /// <remarks>
  1000	        /// If the <see cref="Converter"/> property is set, this method returns it. Otherwise, it returns the default converter from <see cref="GetDefaultConverter"/>.
  1001	        /// If no converter is available, an <see cref="InvalidOperationException"/> is thrown.
  1002	        /// </remarks>
  1003	        /// <returns>
  1004	        /// An <see cref="IConverter{T, U}"/> instance for value conversion.
  1005	        /// </returns>
  1006	        /// <exception cref="InvalidOperationException">
  1007	        /// Thrown if neither <see cref="Converter"/> nor <see cref="GetDefaultConverter"/> provide a non-null converter.
  1008	        /// </exception>
  1009	        protected internal IConverter<T?, U?> GetConverter()
  1010	        {
  1011	            if (Converter is not null)
  1012	            {
  1013	                return Converter;
  1014	            }
  1015	
  1016	            // Cached default converter
  1017	            return _defaultConverter ??= GetDefaultConverter() ?? throw new InvalidOperationException(
  1018	                $"{nameof(GetDefaultConverter)} must return a non-null IConverter.");
  1019	        }
  1020	
  1021	        private void HandleConversionResult<TResult>(ConversionResult<TResult?> result)
  1022	        {
  1023	            if (result.Success)
  1024	            {
  1025	                return;
  1026	            }
  1027	
  1028	            if (result.ErrorMessageKey is not null)
  1029	            {
  1030	                OnConversionError(result.ErrorMessageKey, result.ErrorMessageArgs);
  1031	            }
  1032	            else
  1033	            {
  1034	                OnConversionError(result.ExceptionError.Message, []);
  1035	            }
  1036	        }
  1037	
  1038	        private void OnConversionError(string error, object[] arguments)
  1039	        {
  1040	            // note: we need to update the form here because the conversion error might lead to not updating the value
  1041	            // ... which leads to not updating the form
  1042	            Touched = true;
  1043	            Form?.Update(this);
  1044	            OnConversionErrorOccurred(Localizer[error, arguments]);
  1045	        }
  1046	
  1047	        private void InjectCultureAndFormatToConverter(Func<CultureInfo> culture, Func<string?> format)
  1048	        {
  1049	            if (GetConverter() is ICultureAwareConverter cultureAwareConverter)
  1050	            {
  1051	                cultureAwareConverter.Culture = culture;
  1052	                cultureAwareConverter.Format = format;
  1053	            }
  1054	        }
  1055	
  1056	        protected void ResetConverterErrors()
  1057	        {
  1058	            _getConversionResult = null;
  1059	            _setConversionResult = null;
  1060	        }
  1061	
  1062	        protected internal virtual T? ReadValue => _value;
  1063	
  1064	        protected virtual Task SetValueCoreAsync(T? value)
  1065	        {
  1066	            _value = value;
  1067	
  1068	            return Task.CompletedTask;
  1069	        }
  1070	
  1071	        /// <summary>
  1072	        /// Called to dispose this instance.
  1073	        /// </summary>
  1074	        protected virtual ValueTask DisposeAsyncCore() => ValueTask.CompletedTask;
  1075	
  1076	        /// <inheritdoc />
  1077	        public async ValueTask DisposeAsync()
  1078	        {
  1079	            try
  1080	            {
  1081	                Form?.Remove(this);
  1082	            }
  1083	            catch
  1084	            {
  1085	                // ignored
  1086	            }
  1087	
  1088	            DetachValidationStateChangedListener();
  1089	            await DisposeAsyncCore();
  1090	            GC.SuppressFinalize(this);
  1091	        }
  1092	    }
  1093	}

So in `MudTextField`, the `OnBlur` is set to `OnBlurredAsync` which calls `base.OnBlurredAsync` (from `MudBaseInput`). That's the key - `MudBaseInput.OnBlurredAsync` sets `Touched = true` and calls `ValidateValue()`.

Now let's look at how `MudSelect` handles blur. Looking at `MudSelect.razor`, line 51 shows `OnBlur="@OnBlurAsync"`. Let me check what `OnBlurAsync` does:

Looking at the code-behind `MudSelect.razor.cs` line 1364-1367:

```csharp
internal Task OnBlurAsync(FocusEventArgs obj)
{
    return base.OnBlur.InvokeAsync(obj);
}
```

This only invokes the `OnBlur` EventCallback - it does NOT call `base.OnBlurredAsync` which is the method that sets `Touched = true` and triggers validation. That's the bug!

So `MudInput` uses `@onblur="@OnBlurredAsync"` directly, which calls `MudBaseInput.OnBlurredAsync`. This sets `Touched = true` and calls `ValidateValue()`.

Now for `MudSelect`, the blur handler is set on the outer div at line 6:
```
<div class="@OuterClassname" id="@ElementId" @onfocusout="@OnFocusOutAsync" ...
```

And the `MudInput` inside has `OnBlur="@OnBlurAsync"` at line 51.

The `OnFocusOutAsync` only re-focuses when the menu is open. The `OnBlurAsync` just fires the event callback but doesn't call `base.OnBlurredAsync`.

The fix should be to make `MudSelect.OnBlurAsync` also call `base.OnBlurredAsync` (or at least set `Touched = true` and validate). Let me look at how `MudSelect.OnBlurAsync` is currently implemented and fix it.

Let me also check if there is a similar issue with the `OnFocusOut` handler or if `OnBlurAsync` needs to handle this:

Looking at `MudSelect.razor` line 6:
```
<div class="@OuterClassname" id="@ElementId" @onfocusout="@OnFocusOutAsync" @onclick:stopPropagation @onmousedown:stopPropagation>
```

The `@onfocusout` calls `OnFocusOutAsync` which only handles the case when the menu is open (refocuses). The `OnBlurAsync` is called on the inner `MudInput` component. 

The fix: `MudSelect.OnBlurAsync` should call `base.OnBlurredAsync` to set `Touched = true` and trigger validation.

But wait, there's a subtlety. The `MudSelect` has `SubscribeToParentForm` set to `false` at the top level (line 5 of the razor file), but it cascades `SubscribeToParentForm` to `false` to the inner components. Let me check if `base.OnBlurredAsync` requires `SubscribeToParentForm` to be true.

Looking at `MudBaseInput.ValidateValue()` (line 624-631):
```csharp
protected override async Task ValidateValue()
{
    if (SubscribeToParentForm)
    {
        _validated = true;
        await base.ValidateValue();
    }
}
```

So `MudBaseInput` only validates if `SubscribeToParentForm` is true. The cascading value `SubscribeToParentForm` is set to `false` at the top of `MudSelect.razor` line 5. But the `MudSelect` itself inherits from `MudBaseInput<T>`, and its own `SubscribeToParentForm` property should be `true` by default (since it gets it from the parent form context).

Wait, let me re-read the razor file. At line 5:
```
<CascadingValue Name="SubscribeToParentForm" Value="false" IsFixed="true">
```

This sets `SubscribeToParentForm` to `false` for all children of the select. But the `MudSelect` component itself (the outer component) should still have `SubscribeToParentForm = true` since it's not inside this cascading value.

So when `OnBlurAsync` is called, it should call `base.OnBlurredAsync` which calls `ValidateValue` on the `MudSelect` itself, which checks `SubscribeToParentForm` and if it's true (which it should be for the select itself), it proceeds with validation.

Let me verify this by looking at the class hierarchy. `MudSelect<T>` inherits from `MudBaseInput<T>`, which inherits from `MudFormComponent<T, string>`.

Now let me check if there might be another issue. Let me look at the `OnBlur` callback in the razor file more carefully:

Line 51: `OnBlur="@OnBlurAsync"`

And `OnBlurAsync` is defined in `MudSelect.razor.cs` at line 1364-1367:
```csharp
internal Task OnBlurAsync(FocusEventArgs obj)
{
    return base.OnBlur.InvokeAsync(obj);
}
```

This only invokes the event callback. It should also do what `MudBaseInput.OnBlurredAsync` does - set `Touched = true` and trigger validation. 

Now let me also check if there's a `OnBlurredAsync` that should be called instead. The fix should be:

```csharp
internal Task OnBlurAsync(FocusEventArgs obj)
{
    return base.OnBlurredAsync(obj);
}
```

Wait, but `base.OnBlurredAsync` also invokes `OnBlur` event callback. Let me re-read it:

```csharp
protected internal virtual async Task OnBlurredAsync(FocusEventArgs obj)
{
    _isFocused = false;

    if (ReadOnly)
    {
        return;
    }

    if (!OnlyValidateIfDirty || _isDirty)
    {
        Touched = true;
        if (_validated)
        {
            if (OnBlur.HasDelegate)
            {
                obj.Type += ".additional";
                await OnBlur.InvokeAsync(obj);
            }
        }
        else
        {
            if (OnBlur.HasDelegate)
            {
                obj.Type += ".additional";
                await BeginValidationAfterAsync(OnBlur.InvokeAsync(obj));
            }
            else
            {
                await ValidateValue();
            }
        }
    }
}
```

Yes, `OnBlurredAsync` sets `Touched = true` and calls `ValidateValue()` (or `BeginValidationAfterAsync` if there's an `OnBlur` delegate). It also invokes the `OnBlur` EventCallback.

So the fix is straightforward: change `MudSelect.OnBlurAsync` to call `base.OnBlurredAsync` instead of just invoking `base.OnBlur`.

But wait, I should also check if there's an issue with the `@onfocusout` handler. The outer div has `@onfocusout="@OnFocusOutAsync"` which currently only re-focuses when the menu is open. But blur on the outer div may also need to trigger validation. Let me think about this...

Actually, looking at the flow:
1. The `MudInput` component inside `MudSelect` has `OnBlur="@OnBlurAsync"` 
2. When the input loses focus, `OnBlurAsync` is called
3. `OnBlurAsync` should validate

But also there's `@onfocusout` on the outer div. That's a different event. Let me check if `@onfocusout` on the outer div would also fire when the inner input loses focus (bubbling). It might. But the `OnFocusOutAsync` only handles the case when the menu is open (refocuses). It doesn't trigger validation.

Let me focus on the main issue: changing `OnBlurAsync` to call `base.OnBlurredAsync`.

Actually, wait - let me also check if `OnFocusOutAsync` needs to be modified. When the menu is closed and the user tabs out, the `OnFocusOutAsync` is called. It currently only re-focuses if the menu is open. But it doesn't trigger validation. However, the `OnBlur` on the `MudInput` should still fire... Let me think about this more carefully.

When the select is focused and the user tabs out:
1. First, the `MudInput` (which is a `MudInput<string>`) loses focus, triggering its `@onblur` which calls `OnBlurredAsync` on the `MudInput` instance. But wait, the `MudInput` is a sub-component with `SubscribeToParentForm = false` (because of the cascading value), so its `ValidateValue` won't do anything.
2. Then `OnBlurAsync` on `MudSelect` is called (set as `OnBlur` parameter on the `MudInput`). This should trigger validation on the `MudSelect`.

So the fix is to make `OnBlurAsync` do what `OnBlurredAsync` does.

2026-05-29 10:04:21,933 - INFO - swea-agent - 💭 THOUGHT
Interesting! So `MudAutocomplete` deliberately does NOT validate on blur (line 1152-1154 comment). But `MudSelect` should validate on blur - its current implementation just calls `base.OnBlur.InvokeAsync(obj)` without doing validation. 

Now let me check the `OnBlurAsync` in `MudSelect` more carefully. The method is:

```csharp
internal Task OnBlurAsync(FocusEventArgs obj)
{
    return base.OnBlur.InvokeAsync(obj);
}
```

It should be:

```csharp
internal async Task OnBlurAsync(FocusEventArgs obj)
{
    await base.OnBlurredAsync(obj);
}
```

But wait, there's also `OnFocusOutAsync` which is called on the outer div. Let me check if we also need to handle the case where focus leaves the outer div directly (without going through the inner input). The outer div has `@onfocusout="@OnFocusOutAsync"`. Currently it only re-focuses when the menu is open.

However, I think the main issue is that `OnBlurAsync` doesn't call `base.OnBlurredAsync`. Let me make that change.

But first, let me also check whether there are any related tests or if there's something else I'm missing. Let me look at the `CloseMenu` method to see how it handles blur:

```csharp
public async Task CloseMenu(bool focusAgain = true)
{
    await _openState.SetValueAsync(false);
    UpdateIcon();
    if (focusAgain)
    {
        StateHasChanged();
        await OnBlur.InvokeAsync(new FocusEventArgs());
        _elementReference.FocusAsync().CatchAndLog(ignoreExceptions: true);
        StateHasChanged();
    }
    //...
}
```

When `focusAgain` is true, it invokes `OnBlur` and then refocuses. This is for when the menu closes and focus should return to the select. But it doesn't validate.

Now let me create a fix. The key change is in `MudSelect.razor.cs`:

Change:
```csharp
internal Task OnBlurAsync(FocusEventArgs obj)
{
    return base.OnBlur.InvokeAsync(obj);
}
```

To:
```csharp
internal Task OnBlurAsync(FocusEventArgs obj)
{
    return base.OnBlurredAsync(obj);
}
```

But wait, I need to also consider `OnFocusOutAsync`. When the user tabs out, the order of events is:
1. `@onfocusout` on the outer div fires -> `OnFocusOutAsync` is called
2. If the menu is not open, `OnFocusOutAsync` does nothing
3. The blur on the inner `MudInput` fires -> `OnBlurAsync` is called

So `OnBlurAsync` should handle the validation.

But hold on, there could be another issue. Let me look at the `MudInput` that's used inside `MudSelect`. The `MudInput` has `@onblur="@OnBlurredAsync"` in its own template (line 62 of `MudInput.razor`). But the `MudSelect` passes `OnBlur="@OnBlurAsync"` as a parameter to `MudInput`. The `MudInput` component uses `OnBlur` as an EventCallback parameter. Let me check how `MudInput` handles its own `OnBlur` parameter vs its internal `@onblur`:

Looking at `MudInput.razor.cs`:

   181	        private Task OnInputOrOnChangeAsync(string? input) => Immediate ? OnInput(input) : OnChange(input);
   182	
   183	        protected async Task OnInput(string? args)
   184	        {
   185	            _isFocused = true;
   186	            _internalText = args;
   187	            await OnInternalInputChanged.InvokeAsync(args);
   188	            await SetTextAndUpdateValueAsync(args);
   189	        }
   190	
   191	        protected async Task OnChange(string? args)
   192	        {
   193	            _internalText = args;
   194	            await OnInternalInputChanged.InvokeAsync(args);
   195	            await SetTextAndUpdateValueAsync(args);
   196	        }

   199	        /// Paste hook for descendants.
   200	        /// </summary>
   201	        protected virtual Task OnPaste(ClipboardEventArgs args)
   202	        {
   203	            return Task.CompletedTask;
   204	        }

   206	        /// <inheritdoc />
   207	        public override async ValueTask FocusAsync()
   208	        {
   209	            try
   210	            {
   211	                if (InputType == InputType.Hidden && ChildContent != null)
   212	                    await _elementReference1.FocusAsync();
   213	                else
   214	                    await ElementReference.FocusAsync();
   215	            }
   216	            catch (Exception e)
   217	            {
   218	                Console.WriteLine($@"MudInput.FocusAsync: {e.Message}");
   219	            }
   220	        }
   221	
   222	        /// <inheritdoc />
   223	        public override ValueTask BlurAsync()
   224	        {
   225	            return ElementReference.MudBlurAsync();
   226	        }

   239	
   240	        private Size GetButtonSize() => Margin == Margin.Dense ? Size.Small : Size.Medium;
   241	
   242	        /// <summary>
   243	        /// Determine whether to show the clear button when Clearable==true.
   244	        /// Of course the clear button won't show up if the text field is empty
   245	        /// </summary>
   246	        private bool ShowClearButton()
   247	        {
   248	            if (GetDisabledState())
   249	            {
   250	                return false;
   251	            }
   252	
   253	            if (!Clearable)
   254	            {
   255	                return false;
   256	            }
   257	
   258	            // If this is a standalone input it will not be clearable when read-only
   259	            if (SubscribeToParentForm && GetReadOnlyState())
   260	            {
   261	                return false;
   262	            }
   263	
   264	            if (ReadValue is string stringValue)
   265	            {
   266	                return !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(stringValue);
   267	            }
   268	
   269	            return ReadValue is not string and not null;
   270	        }
   271	
   272	        protected virtual async Task HandleClearButtonAsync(MouseEventArgs e)
   273	        {
   274	            await SetTextAndUpdateValueAsync(string.Empty, updateValue: true);
   275	            await ElementReference.FocusAsync();
   276	            await OnClearButtonClick.InvokeAsync(e);
   277	        }
   278	
   279	        protected virtual async Task HandleSpinButtonPointerDownAsync()
   280	        {
   281	            await ElementReference.FocusAsync();
   282	        }
   283	
   284	        private readonly record struct AutoSizingVisualState(
   285	            Variant Variant,
   286	            Margin Margin,
   287	            Typo Typo,
   288	            Adornment Adornment,
   289	            string? Class,
   290	            string? Style,
   291	            bool Disabled);
   292	
   293	        private AutoSizingVisualState CaptureAutoSizingVisualState()
   294	            => new(Variant, Margin, Typo, Adornment, Class, Style, GetDisabledState());
   295	
   296	        private void ResetAutoSizingFlags()
   297	        {
   298	            _shouldInitSizing = false;
   299	            _shouldUpdateSizingParams = false;
   300	            _shouldAdjustSizingAfterRender = false;
   301	        }
   302	
   303	        private void SyncAutoSizingTextSnapshot()
   304	        {
   305	            _oldText = _internalText;
   306	        }
   307	
   308	        /// <inheritdoc />
   309	        public override async Task SetParametersAsync(ParameterView parameters)
   310	        {
   311	            // Visual/style-affecting changes.
   312	            var oldVisualState = CaptureAutoSizingVisualState();
   313	
   314	            // Handled separately because they drive different lifecycle actions.
   315	            var oldLines = Lines;
   316	            var oldMaxLines = MaxLines;
   317	            var oldSizing = Sizing;
   318	
   319	            await base.SetParametersAsync(parameters);
   320	
   321	            var newSizing = Sizing;
   322	            var hasAutoSizingVisualChange = oldVisualState != CaptureAutoSizingVisualState();
   323	            var hasAutoSizingParameterChange = oldLines != Lines || oldMaxLines != MaxLines || oldSizing != newSizing;
   324	
   325	            // Always update internal text (TextUpdateSuppression removed)
   326	            _internalText = ReadText;
   327	
   328	            if (oldSizing == InputSizing.Fixed && newSizing != InputSizing.Fixed)
   329	            {
   330	                _shouldInitSizing = true;
   331	            }
   332	
   333	            if (newSizing != InputSizing.Fixed && !_shouldInitSizing && hasAutoSizingVisualChange)
   334	            {
   335	                // Re-measure after style/class-related updates because runtime classes and computed styles can affect textarea metrics.
   336	                _shouldAdjustSizingAfterRender = true;
   337	            }
   338	
   339	            if (oldSizing != InputSizing.Fixed && newSizing == InputSizing.Fixed)
   340	            {
   341	                // Disable dynamic sizing.
   342	                ResetAutoSizingFlags();
   343	                if (IsJSRuntimeAvailable)
   344	                {
   345	                    await JsRuntime.InvokeVoidAsyncWithErrorHandling("mudInputSizing.destroy", ElementReference);
   346	                }
   347	            }
   348	            else if (newSizing != InputSizing.Fixed && !_shouldInitSizing && hasAutoSizingParameterChange)
   349	            {
   350	                // Defer until OnAfterRender so measurements use the latest DOM/classes.
   351	                _shouldUpdateSizingParams = true;
   352	            }
   353	        }
   354	
   355	        [Inject]
   356	        private IJSRuntime JsRuntime { get; set; } = null!;
   357	
   358	        protected override async Task OnAfterRenderAsync(bool firstRender)
   359	        {
   360	            if (Sizing != InputSizing.Fixed)
   361	            {
   362	                if (firstRender || _shouldInitSizing)
   363	                {
   364	                    ResetAutoSizingFlags();
   365	                    await JsRuntime.InvokeVoidAsyncWithErrorHandling("mudInputSizing.init", ElementReference, MaxLines);
   366	                    SyncAutoSizingTextSnapshot();
   367	                }
   368	                else if (_shouldUpdateSizingParams)
   369	                {
   370	                    _shouldUpdateSizingParams = false;
   371	                    _shouldAdjustSizingAfterRender = false;
   372	                    await JsRuntime.InvokeVoidAsyncWithErrorHandling("mudInputSizing.updateParams", ElementReference, MaxLines);
   373	                    SyncAutoSizingTextSnapshot();
   374	                }
   375	                else if (_shouldAdjustSizingAfterRender || _oldText != _internalText)
   376	                {
   377	                    _shouldAdjustSizingAfterRender = false;
   378	                    await JsRuntime.InvokeVoidAsyncWithErrorHandling("mudInputSizing.adjustHeight", ElementReference);
   379	                    SyncAutoSizingTextSnapshot();
   380	                }
   381	            }
   382	            if (firstRender)
   383	            {
   384	                // add onblur event through javascript which will trigger CallOnBlurredAsync
   385	                // must do in javascript or it won't detect ios Keyboard button - limitation of Blazor/React/other frameworks of the DOM
   386	                await ElementReference.MudAttachBlurEventWithJS(_dotNetReferenceLazy.Value);
   387	            }
   388	
   389	            await base.OnAfterRenderAsync(firstRender);
   390	        }
   391	
   392	        /// <summary>
   393	        /// Set the <see cref="MudBaseInput{T}.Text"/> to the specified value.
   394	        /// </summary>
   395	        /// <param name="text">The new value.</param>
   396	        public Task SetText(string? text)
   397	        {
   398	            return SetText(text, updateValue: true);
   399	        }
   400	
   401	        internal Task SetText(string? text, bool updateValue)
   402	        {
   403	            _internalText = text;
   404	            return SetTextAndUpdateValueAsync(text, updateValue);
   405	        }
   406	
   407	        // Certain HTML5 inputs (dates and color) have a native placeholder
   408	        private bool HasNativeHtmlPlaceholder()
   409	        {
   410	            return GetInputType()
   411	                is InputType.Color
   412	                or InputType.Date
   413	                or InputType.DateTimeLocal
   414	                or InputType.Month
   415	                or InputType.Time
   416	                or InputType.Week;
   417	        }
   418	
   419	        /// <inheritdoc />
   420	        protected override async ValueTask DisposeAsyncCore()
   421	        {
   422	            if (IsJSRuntimeAvailable)
   423	            {
   424	                await JsRuntime.InvokeVoidAsyncWithErrorHandling("mudElementRef.removeOnBlurEvent", ElementReference);
   425	                if (Sizing != InputSizing.Fixed)
   426	                {
   427	                    await JsRuntime.InvokeVoidAsyncWithErrorHandling("mudInputSizing.destroy", ElementReference);
   428	                }
   429	            }
   430	
   431	            if (_dotNetReferenceLazy.IsValueCreated)
   432	            {
   433	                _dotNetReferenceLazy.Value.Dispose();
   434	            }
   435	
   436	            await base.DisposeAsyncCore();
   437	        }
   438	
   439	        [JSInvokable]
   440	        public async Task CallOnBlurredAsync()
   441	        {
   442	            // If onblurred already fired then cancel
   443	            if (!_isFocused)
   444	                return;
   445	
   446	            await OnBlurredAsync(new FocusEventArgs { Type = "jsBlur.OnBlur" });
   447	        }
   448	    }

   453	    public class MudInputString : MudInput<string> { }
